pdf-text-extract.notes: When model successfully loads skill and uses tools, scores 100. ~33-50% of runs, devstral-small enters text-generation loop (65536 tokens, 0 tool calls) instead of making tool calls.

pdf-merge-info.notes: Model successfully merges PDFs and extracts page counts. Titles extracted via text (iteration 5). Authors still extracted from PDF metadata (empty) instead of text content. Text-loop issue affects ~33% of runs.

changes_made.change: Initial targeted skill with bundled Python scripts (extract_pdf_text.py, merge_pdfs.py, pdf_page_count.py) in scripts/ directory. Detailed workflow instructions for extraction and merging.
Simplified skill description to reduce model's compulsion to load skill. Shortened SKILL.md body.

Changed skill name from 'nano-pdf' to 'pdf' to match successful document-pdf pattern. Removed scripts directory. Matched document-pdf skill structure (inline Python examples). This was the breakthrough - model started successfully loading skill and using tools.
Added table parsing guidance for regional data extraction. Added pypdf merge example. Removed 'merge' from description to reduce skill-loading compulsion for merge tasks.
Added explicit 'Extract Document Info' section showing how to get titles/authors from TEXT content (not PDF metadata). Emphasized that PDF metadata is often empty.

key_findings: devstral-small has a fundamental issue with the bare agent's <load-skill> XML tag mechanism: it garbles the tag ~33-50% of the time, producing text like 'nano-pdf}' or 'load-skill>nano-pdf</skill>' instead of '<load-skill>pdf</load-skill>'
When skill loading fails, the model enters a text-generation loop (65536 tokens) without making any tool calls
Changing the skill name from 'nano-pdf' to 'pdf' dramatically improved success rate (matching the successful document-pdf skill pattern)
Removing bundled scripts and using inline code examples was more effective than providing external scripts
The model successfully uses pdftotext CLI, pdfplumber, and pypdf when it does engage tool-calling mode
The text-loop issue appears to be a model-level behavior, not addressable through skill content changes
